subtopic: Specialized Medical Therapies and Stem Cells
title: Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-cell (CAR-T) Therapy
focus: Mechanism, advantages, and challenges of CAR-T cell therapy.
rationale: CAR-T is a major breakthrough in cancer treatment. UPSC often asks about the mechanism of such biotechnological advancements and their specific applications or side effects like Cytokine Release Syndrome.
sectionExcerpt: CAR-T therapy involves collecting a patient's T cells, genetically modifying them in a lab to add a Chimeric Antigen Receptor (CAR), and infusing them back to attack cancer cells. It is a living drug offering long-term remission and personalized treatment. Challenges include Cytokine Release Syndrome and Antigen Escape.
evidenceBullets: CDSCO approved Qartemi, a CAR T-cell therapy for treating blood cancer.
Patient's own T cells are genetically modified with CAR to recognize and attack cancer cells.
Advantages include long-term remission, personalized treatment, and short treatment duration of approximately 45 days.
Challenges include Cytokine Release Syndrome (a systemic inflammatory response) and Antigen Escape (tumors lose targeted antigens).

subtopic: Specialized Medical Therapies and Stem Cells
title: Mitochondrial Replacement Therapy (MRT)
focus: Concept of mitochondrial diseases, maternal inheritance, and the MRT/Three-Parent Baby IVF technique.
rationale: MRT involves complex genetic concepts like maternal inheritance of mitochondrial DNA and three-parent babies, which are highly relevant for UPSC Prelims Science & Tech questions.
sectionExcerpt: Mitochondrial diseases are exclusively inherited from the mother and affect cellular energy production. MRT is an IVF technique that replaces a woman's abnormal mitochondrial DNA with a donor's healthy one. The resulting embryo contains nuclear DNA from the parents and mitochondria from a donor, creating a Three-Parent Baby. Techniques include Pronuclear Transfer (PNT) and Maternal Spindles Transfer (MST).
evidenceBullets: Mitochondria generate over 90% of cellular energy and have their own DNA, distinct from nuclear DNA.
Mitochondrial diseases are exclusively inherited from the child's mother.
MRT combines parents' nuclear material with a donor's healthy mitochondria.
The child born has three distinct genetic materials, termed a Three-Parent Baby.
Techniques include Pronuclear Transfer (done after fertilization) and Maternal Spindles Transfer (done before fertilization).

subtopic: Specialized Medical Therapies and Stem Cells
title: Stem Cells and their Types
focus: Definition, properties, and classification of stem cells into Pluripotent and Adult types.
rationale: Stem cells are a foundational biotechnology topic. The distinction between embryonic, induced pluripotent, and adult stem cells is a classic area for conceptual UPSC questions.
sectionExcerpt: Stem cells are unspecialized cells capable of self-renewal and differentiation. They are categorized into Pluripotent stem cells (Embryonic Stem Cells and Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells) which can differentiate into all adult body cells, and Adult/Somatic Stem Cells which yield specialized cell types of their specific tissue or organ.
evidenceBullets: Stem cells can self-renew indefinitely and differentiate into mature, specialized cells.
Pluripotent stem cells can differentiate into all cells of the adult body.
Embryonic Stem Cells (ESCs) are found in the early embryo 3-5 days post-fertilization.
Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells (iPSCs) are created by introducing embryonic genes into a somatic cell (e.g., skin cell) to revert it to a stem cell state.
Adult Stem Cells (ASCs) are undifferentiated cells in tissues or organs that yield specialized cells of that specific tissue.

subtopic: Specialized Medical Therapies and Stem Cells
title: Animal Stem Cell Biobank
focus: India's first animal stem cell biobank and its supporting mission.
rationale: First-of-its-kind scientific infrastructure in India. Good for factual prelims questions regarding the location (NIAB) and supporting body (NBM).
sectionExcerpt: India's first state-of-the-art animal stem cell biobank and laboratory was inaugurated at the National Institute of Animal Biotechnology (NIAB), Hyderabad. It focuses on regenerative medicine and cellular therapies for livestock and is supported by the National Biopharma Mission (NBM).
evidenceBullets: Located at the National Institute of Animal Biotechnology (NIAB), Hyderabad.
Focuses on regenerative medicine, cellular therapies for livestock, and disease modeling.
Supported by the National Biopharma Mission (NBM) under the Department of Biotechnology - BIRAC.
NBM is an industry-academia collaborative mission for biopharmaceutical development.

subtopic: Specialized Medical Therapies and Stem Cells
title: Phenome India National Biobank
focus: Inauguration and location of the Phenome India National Biobank.
rationale: Very brief mention, but distinct from the animal biobank. Suitable for a simple matching or factual question.
sectionExcerpt: The Phenome India National Biobank was recently inaugurated at the CSIR-Institute of Genomics and Integrative Biology (IGIB).
evidenceBullets: Inaugurated at the CSIR-Institute of Genomics and Integrative Biology (IGIB).
